findings.description: No candidate rules exist for this blob at all — the entire 'Graphic Elements — Waves' section (colorway definitions, construction/layering spec, usage guidance, and masthead gradient device) has not been clustered into any brand_rule rows. This is a total extraction miss, not just a split/merge issue.

rules.summary: Wave graphic colorways: Sunshine/Gold and Sky Blue/Deep Blue, each in long and short variants.
Wave construction: top layer at 70% transparency over base wave, must preserve layered translucent quality.
Wave usage: standalone or overlaid on photography/gradients to support flow, frame content, or add depth; only approved color values permitted.
Masthead gradient with logo mark (Sunshine to 0C,10M,78Y,0K, logo at lightest end) is the approved device for masthead copy holding.

findings.description: The construction rule uses the imperative 'always preserve the layered, translucent quality' — a hard compliance requirement — but no rule/governance facet (hardness=hard, polarity=must) has been captured since no rule exists at all.

'Approved color values only' is a restrictive compliance clause (scope-limiting) that should carry explicit polarity=must/restricted and reference to an approved palette governance artifact, but this facet is absent since no rule captures it.

Missed relation: the masthead gradient device explicitly starts from 'Sunshine', one of the two wave colorways defined in u_0001, indicating a cross-reference between the wave colorway rule and the masthead gradient rule that should be linked (e.g., relates_to) rather than treated as fully independent.
